quality.rationale: 基于时空几何与洛伦兹Boost的基础证明题，逻辑链较短但题干与解答完整清晰

origin.question: # Problem 3.1.(I) If 2 events are separated by a spacelike interval, show that(a) there exists a Lorentz frame in which they are simultaneous, and
origin.answer: 存在一个沿空间分离方向、速度为 $\beta = \Delta t / \Delta x$ 的洛伦兹参考系，使得两事件的坐标时间差为零（即同时发生）。
origin.solution: Solution 3.1. The neatest way to do this problem is by using spacetime diagrams.First rotate the coordinate system until both events lie on the $x$ -axis and the first event occurs at $x = t = 0$ .The two events A and B at spacelike separation $(\Delta x, \Delta t)$ are depicted in Figure (i), with the world line of a light ray indicated as the dotted $45^{\circ}$ line. A Lorentz transformation, shown as the $t'$ , $x'$ axes in Figure (ii), can obviously be made to make A and B simultaneous. (The magnitude of the Lorentz boost is $\beta = \Delta t / \Delta x$ and $\tan h^{-1} \tan \theta$ in Figure (ii) is the rapidity parameter.) Since the $t'$ axis cannot be rotated below the light cone line in a Lorentz transformation, the two events cannot be made to occur at the same location.![](images/791ecbfd27d2788c0e5a39f075496c4b2c65e997ccf440f8bc19a88ca68e07d.jpg)(i)![](images/0f42dbaee42556a34297defb402858d1bd552289fe2c80edc8d51efb936fce70.jpg)(ii)![](images/39204ea8db2f4e62cbf70baa508d25bbb11dc1a1581a2204e6151381555b7878.jpg)(iii)![](images/c29204ea8db2f4e62cbf70baa508d25bbb11dc1a1581a2204e6151381555b7878.jpg)(iv)Figure (iii) depicts events A and B at timelike separation. As shown in Figure (iv), a Lorentz transformation can obviously be made to bring A and B to the same location in the primed frame. The magnitude of the required boost is $\alpha = \Delta x / \Delta t$ . Since the $x'$ axis cannot be rotated above the light cone line, the two events cannot be made simultaneous.
